title: hyptis coccinea
intro: Hyptis coccinea, also known as red bush or red hyptis, is a tropical plant species native to Central and South America, belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Hyptis coccinea has been associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and reduce fever. The plant can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and its leaves can also be used in traditional medicine, often in combination with other herbs, to create tonics and remedies.
